Bases: UserAdmin

fieldsets = ((None, {'fields': ('username', 'password')}), ('Personal info', {'fields': ('first_name', 'last_name', 'email')}), ('Permissions', {'fields': ('is_active', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser', 'groups', 'user_permissions')}), ('Important dates', {'fields': ('last_login', 'date_joined')}), ('Role & Publisher', {'fields': ('role', 'publisher', 'subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists')}))¶
property media¶
news_app.admin.setup_groups(modeladmin, request, queryset)[source]¶

news_app.api_views module¶

class news_app.api_views.SubscribedArticlesAPIView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ListAPIView

get_queryset()[source]¶

Get the list of items for this view. This must be an iterable, and may be a queryset. Defaults to using self.queryset.

This method should always be used rather than accessing self.queryset directly, as self.queryset gets evaluated only once, and those results are cached for all subsequent requests.

You may want to override this if you need to provide different querysets depending on the incoming request.

(Eg. return a list of items that is specific to the user)

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

ready()[source]¶

news_app.models module¶

class news_app.models.Article(id, title, content, date_posted, author, publisher, status, approved_by)[source]¶

DRAFT = 'draft'¶

PENDING = 'pending'¶
REJECTED = 'rejected'¶
STATUS_CHOICES = [('draft', 'Draft'), ('pending', 'Pending Review'), ('approved', 'Approved'), ('rejected', 'Rejected')]¶

exception NotUpdated¶

articles¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

subscribers¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

save(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Save the current instance. Override this in a subclass if you want to control the saving process.

The ‘force_insert’ and ‘force_update’ parameters can be used to insist that the “save” must be an SQL insert or update (or equivalent for non-SQL backends), respectively. Normally, they should not be set.

subscribed_journalists¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

news_app.signals module¶

news_app.signals.assign_user_to_group(sender, instance, created, **kwargs)[source]¶

Keep Django auth groups synchronized with the user’s selected role.

news_app.signals.create_groups_and_permissions()[source]¶

Create role groups and attach the expected article permissions.

news_app.signals.ensure_groups_after_migrate(sender, **kwargs)[source]¶

Automatically create/update role groups and permissions after migrations.

news_app.signals.notify_subscribers_on_approval(sender, instance, **kwargs)[source]¶

Notify subscribed readers when an article is approved.

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

Bases: TestCase

Validate account registration behavior from the public signup page.

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_reader_subscribed_to_publisher_sees_publisher_article()[source]¶

news_app.urls module¶

news_app.views module¶

news_app.views.approve_article(request, pk)[source]¶

Approve or reject a pending article, editor-only.

news_app.views.article_detail(request, pk)[source]¶

Show a single approved article to authenticated users.

news_app.views.editor_dashboard(request)[source]¶

List pending articles for editors to review.

news_app.views.ensure_demo_accounts()[source]¶

Ensure role demo accounts exist with known credentials for evaluation.

news_app.views.home(request)[source]¶

Render the public homepage with only approved articles.

news_app.views.journalist_dashboard(request)[source]¶

Display journalist-owned articles; deny access to non-journalists.

news_app.views.login_view(request)[source]¶

Authenticate a user with username/password and start a session.

news_app.views.logout_view(request)[source]¶

End the current session and send the user to the login page.

news_app.views.reader_dashboard(request)[source]¶

Display user-specific content for readers.

news_app.views.setup_permissions_view(request)[source]¶

Create default role groups/permissions, superuser-only utility endpoint.

news_app.views.signup_view(request)[source]¶

Create a new user account with a specific role.

Expected POST fields:

username: Unique username for the new account. email: Required email for the new account. password: Account password. confirm_password: Password confirmation. role: Selected user role (reader, journalist, or editor).

news_app.views.submit_article(request)[source]¶

Allow journalists to submit new articles for editor review.
